backend.api_rest.controllers package¶

Submodules¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.attribute_templates_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.attribute_templates_controller.get_attribute_template_by_id(attribute_template_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.attribute_templates_controller.get_attribute_templates(sort: str, order: str, page: int, limit: int)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller module¶

Calibration Controller¶

This module contains all the functionalities related to the calibration processes. It provides endpoints and background tasks to process calibration and related operations.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.background_mz_fit(filename, calibration_collection_id, ionization_mechanism_ids, peak_intensity_min, isotope_abundance_min, match_score_min, refine_window, sample_item_id)[source]¶

Run mz_fit as a background task asynchronously.

Parameters:

... – parameters.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.calibration_mz_apply(fit: dict, sample_filename: str, autosampler_mode: bool | None = None)[source]¶

Apply m/z calibration to a sample file.

Parameters:

  • fit – Fit dictionary.

  • sample_filename – Name of the sample file.

  • autosampler_mode – Optional flag for autosampler mode. In the calibration.js store affects if the batch will be reloaded onCalibrationMzApplyFinished

Returns:

List of calibrated sample item IDs.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.calibration_mz_calibrate_batch(sample_batch, sample_items, params: CalibrationMzFitParams)[source]¶

Calibrates the entire batch of samples and notifies about the progress and completion using socket IO. In case of failure, an error message is emitted.

Parameters:

  • sample_batch – The batch of samples to be calibrated.

  • sample_items – List of sample items within the batch.

  • params – Calibration parameters defined by the CalibrationMzFitParams pydantic model.

Returns:

A list containing the calibration results for the batch.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.calibration_mz_calibrate_sample(sample_item, params: CalibrationMzFitParams, background_tasks)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.calibration_mz_fit(sample_item_id: str, params: CalibrationMzFitParams, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks | None = None)[source]¶

Start m/z fit calibration for a given sample item based on the calibration parameters. It also manages background tasks and communicates the progress using socket IO.

Parameters:

  • sample_item_id – ID of the sample item.

  • params – Calibration parameters.

  • background_tasks – Optional background task parameter.

Returns:

Status message if called from route endpoint or fit, stats, error if called during automatica sample calibration from the mz_calibrate_sample.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.emit_progress_update(progress_properties, increment)[source]¶

Emit progress updates to the given sample item room.

Parameters:

  • progress_properties – Dictionary containing information about the current item being processed.

  • increment – Float representing the progress percentage increment.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.get_mz_calibration(instrument: str | None = None, sample_item_id: str | None = None)[source]¶

Retrieve the mz calibration for a given instrument or sample item ID.

Parameters:

  • instrument (str, optional) – (Optional) The instrument name.

  • sample_item_id (str, optional) – (Optional) The sample item ID.

Returns:

The mz calibration for the given parameters.

Return type:

dict

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.mz_calibrate_sample(sample_item, params, filename, autosampler_mode: bool | None = None)[source]¶

Calibrates a single sample. It first fits the sample and then applies the calibration. Notifications are sent based on the progress using socket IO.

Parameters:

  • sample_item – The sample item to calibrate.

  • params – Calibration parameters defined by the CalibrationMzFitParams pydantic model.

  • filename – Name of the file corresponding to the sample item.

  • autosampler_mode – Indicates whether the calibration is in autosampler mode or not.

Returns:

The calibration result for the given sample.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.mz_fit(filename, calibration_collection_id, ionization_mechanism_ids, peak_intensity_min, isotope_abundance_min, match_score_min, refine_window, sample_item_id)[source]¶

Main function to fit mz. Fits the mass-to-charge ratio (m/z) for a given sample file.

Parameters:

... – parameters.

Returns:

fit, stats, error.

backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.remove_duplicate_mz_values(mz)[source]¶
backend.api_rest.controllers.calibration_controller.signal_mz_calibration_update(fit, filename)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.ionization_mechanisms_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.ionization_mechanisms_controller.create_ionization_mechanism(ionization_mechanism: IonizationMechanismCreate) → dict[source]¶

Function to create a new ionization mechanism. Generates corresponding ions for each existing target compound in the database.

Parameters:

ionization_mechanism (IonizationMechanismCreate) – Ionization mechanism to create

Raises:

HTTPException – Failed to create the ionization mechanism

Returns:

Created ionization mechanism

Return type:

dict

async backend.api_rest.controllers.ionization_mechanisms_controller.get_ionization_mechanism(ionization_mechanism_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.ionization_mechanisms_controller.get_ionization_mechanisms(ionization_mechanism_polarity: str | None = None, ionization_mechanism: str | None = None, reagent: str | None = None, sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 100)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller module¶

Match Controller

This module contains all the functionalities and endpoints related to the matching/rematching processes and related operations.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.compute_match_interferences(filename, target_isotopes_df) → DataFrame[source]¶

Computes match interferences for a given sample file based on specified target isotopes.

This function calculates the raw intensities for each target isotope within the specified mass-to-charge (m/z) range, which are used to identify potential interferences in the sample’s spectrum. It involves loading the sample file data, summing up the spectrum, and then computing the raw intensities for the target isotopes.

Steps: 1. Load the sample file and compute the summed spectrum across all time points. 2. For each target isotope, calculate the raw intensity within a defined m/z range around the target m/z value.

Parameters:

  • filename (str) – Path to the sample file from which to compute interferences.

  • target_isotopes_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing the target isotopes and their m/z values.

Returns:

DataFrame with computed interferences for each target isotope.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during the computation process.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.compute_matches(filename, target_isotopes_df, min_isotope_abundance=0.15)[source]¶

Computes matches for specified target isotopes within a sample file.

This function identifies the best matching peaks within the sample spectrum for each target isotope based on their m/z values. It computes match statistics such as match score, m/z error, and isotope correlation.

Steps: 1. Load peaks from the sample file and prepare the data for matching. 2. Match each target isotope to the closest peak within a predefined m/z tolerance window. 3. Compute match statistics such as isotope correlations, m/z errors, and match score. 4. Return a DataFrame containing the match details for each target isotope.

Parameters:

  • filename (str) – Path to the sample file to be analyzed for matches.

  • target_isotopes_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing target isotopes with their m/z values and other properties.

  • min_isotope_abundance (float, optional) – Minimum relative abundance threshold for isotopes to be considered in matching.

Returns:

DataFrame with details of the matches found for each target isotope.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during the matching process.

Notes

  • Matching is done on isotope-level. Ion, compound and collection level matches are aggregated from

isotope-level matches on read sample operation; see the samples_controller.py for this aggregation.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.compute_sample_match(sample: MatchComputeSample, target_isotopes_df, progress_properties: ProgressProperties | None = None)[source]¶

Computes matches and match interferences for a given sample against a set of target isotopes.

This function takes a sample and a DataFrame containing target isotopes, computes match interferences, and matches for the sample. It updates the computation progress if progress properties are provided. Matches and interferences are then saved to the database.

Steps: 1. Unpack sample parameters including sample item ID and filename. 2. Compute match interferences for the sample using the provided target isotopes. 3. Compute matches for the sample using the provided target isotopes. 4. Save computed match interferences and matches to the database, ensuring no duplication. 5. Update computation progress at each significant step if progress tracking is enabled.

Parameters:

  • sample (MatchComputeSample) – Contains details of the sample for which matches are being computed, including sample item ID and filename.

  • target_isotopes_df (DataFrame) – A DataFrame containing target isotope information for match computation.

  • progress_properties (ProgressProperties, optional) – Optional parameters for tracking progress of match computation.

Raises:

RuntimeError – If no match interferences or matches are found during computation.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.filter_existing_sample_matches_and_interferences(target_isotopes_df, sample_item_id)[source]¶

Filters out target isotopes for a given sample item that already have matches or match interferences, ensuring that only isotopes without existing matches are considered for new match computation.

This function checks existing matches and match interferences for the given sample item and excludes those target isotopes from the provided DataFrame that already have matches or interferences. This helps in optimizing the match computation process by avoiding redundant calculations for isotopes that already have matches.

Steps: 1. Retrieve existing matches and match interferences for the specified sample item. 2. Identify target isotope IDs from existing matches and interferences. 3. Filter out these isotopes from the provided DataFrame to exclude already matched isotopes. 4. Return the filtered DataFrame, ready for further match computation processes.

Parameters:

  • target_isotopes_df (pandas.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing target isotopes to be considered for match computation.

  • sample_item_id (str) – Unique identifier of the sample item for which existing matches and interferences are to be checked.

Raises:

RuntimeError – Raises an error if the process of fetching existing matches or interferences, or filtering fails.

Returns:

A filtered DataFrame excluding isotopes that already have matches or interferences.

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.match_batch_compute(sample_batch_id: str, added_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, added_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, independent_transaction: bool = False, progress_properties: ProgressProperties | None = None)[source]¶

Computes new matches for all samples within a given batch, taking into account any added target compounds or ionization mechanisms.

This function handles the computation of matches for each sample item in the specified sample batch. It accommodates the inclusion of added target compounds or ionization mechanisms by determining the relevant target isotopes that require match computation. It ensures that redundant computations are avoided by checking for pre-existing matches and match interferences.

Steps: 1. Retrieve all samples associated with the given sample batch. 2. Gather batch-specific information, including ionization mechanisms and target compounds. 3. Determine target isotopes for which new match computation is required (specific to added compounds/ion_mechanisms or for all the batch targets in case of complete rematching). 4. For each sample in the batch, preprocess sample data and check for existing matches. 5. Compute matches for the sample items where new target isotopes are identified. 6. Emit reload event for the batch if this function is called as an independent transaction. 7. If there are any failed samples, raise an exception with the list of failed samples included in the error message

Parameters:

  • sample_batch_id (str) – The identifier of the sample batch for which match computation is to be performed.

  • added_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – A list of identifiers for target compounds that have been added to the batch, limiting the scope of match computation.

  • added_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – A list of identifiers for ionization mechanisms that have been added to the batch, limiting the scope of match computation.

  • independent_transaction (bool, optional) – Indicates whether the match computation operation should be treated as a standalone process, which affects event emission and UI updates.

  • progress_properties (ProgressProperties, optional) – Properties related to the progress tracking of the match computation process, facilitating user feedback.

Raises:

ValueError – Raised in cases where match computation cannot proceed due to issues such as unverified m/z calibration or the absence new target isotopes to compute matches for.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.match_batch_remove(sample_batch_id: str, removed_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, independent_transaction: bool = False)[source]¶

Removes matches associated with a sample batch, optionally filtering by removed target compounds or ionization mechanisms.

This function deletes matches (and associated match interferences) for a given sample batch. If removed target compound IDs or ionization mechanism IDs are provided, the function fetches associated target isotope IDs and deletes matches specific to these isotopes. If no filters are provided, all matches for the batch are deleted.

Steps: 1. Retrieve all sample items associated with the sample batch. 2. Determine the target isotope IDs that are associated with the removed compounds or ionization mechanisms. 3. Execute the deletion of matches and associated interferences based on the identified target isotope IDs or remove all matches if no filters are applied. 4. Emit a reload event for the sample batch if this is an independent transaction.

Parameters:

  • sample_batch_id (str) – ID of the sample batch for which matches are to be removed.

  • removed_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – List of target compound IDs for which matches are to be removed, optional.

  • removed_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – List of ionization mechanism IDs for which matches are to be removed, optional.

  • independent_transaction (bool) – Flag indicating if the operation should be an independent transaction, default to False.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.match_sample_compute(sample_item_id: str, added_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, added_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, independent_transaction: bool = False)[source]¶

Computes new matches for a specific sample item, taking into account any added target compounds or ionization mechanisms.

This function handles the computation of matches for a given sample item. It accommodates the inclusion of added target compounds or ionization mechanisms by determining the relevant target isotopes that require match computation. It ensures that redundant computations are avoided by checking for pre-existing matches and match interferences.

Steps: 1. Gather necessary sample information. 2. Retrieve associated batch information and its ionization mechanisms. 3. Determine target isotopes that require new match computation. 4. Check if there are existing match records for these target isotopes to avoid redundancy. 5. Proceed with match computation if there are target isotopes to process. 6. Emit reload event for the affected batch users if this function is called as an independent transaction.

Parameters:

  • sample_item_id (str) – ID of the sample item for which matches are to be computed.

  • added_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of added target compound IDs to be considered for match computation, defaults to None

  • added_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of added ionization mechanism IDs to be considered for match computation, defaults to None

  • independent_transaction (bool, optional) – Flag to indicate if the operation should be treated as an independent transaction, defaults to False

Raises:

RuntimeError – Raised when no new target isotopes are available for match computation.

TODO: - optimize instrument/compute_sample_match notifications emits/listeners. item compoute for instrument usees (Scenthound), s

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.match_sample_remove(sample_item_id: str, removed_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, independent_transaction: bool = False)[source]¶

Removes matches and match interferences for a specific sample item, potentially filtered by specific target compounds or ionization mechanisms.

This function deletes matches (and associated match interferences) for a given sample item. When provided, filters based on removed target compounds or ionization mechanisms are applied, limiting the deletion to matches associated with those criteria. If no filters are specified, all matches for the sample item are removed. This operation can be performed as part of a larger transaction (rematch_sample endpoint) or as an independent transaction (Postman), in which case a reload event is emitted for the sample batch.

Steps: 1. Identify the sample item for match deletion. 2. If specified, determine the target isotope IDs linked to the removed compounds or ionization mechanisms, which will limit the deletion of related matches. 3. Execute the deletion of matches and associated interferences based on the identified target isotope IDs or remove all matches if no filters are applied. 4. If operating as an independent transaction, emit a reload event for the sample batch to reflect the changes.

Parameters:

  • sample_item_id (str) – Unique identifier for the sample item whose matches are to be removed.

  • removed_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – List of target compound IDs to filter the matches that need to be removed, optional.

  • removed_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – List of ionization mechanism IDs to filter the matches that need to be removed, optional.

  • independent_transaction (bool) – Flag indicating whether the operation should be treated as an independent transaction, defaults to False.

Raises:

  • HTTPException – Raises an HTTPException if the operation fails during an independent transaction.

  • RuntimeError – Raises a RuntimeError for internal call failures when not in an independent transaction.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.rematch_batch(sample_batch_id: str, added_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, added_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, independent_transaction: bool = False, progress_properties: ProgressProperties | None = None)[source]¶

Performs a rematch of sample batch by removing and/or computing matches based on the specified parameters.

This function handles the rematch process of a sample batch by first removing matches associated with removed target compounds or ionization mechanisms and then adding matches for added compounds or mechanisms. If no parameters are provided, it performs a complete rematch by removing all existing sample matches and recomputing them.

Steps: 1. Remove existing matches associated with removed parameters, if specified. 2. Compute new matches for added parameters, if specified. 3. In the absence of specified parameters for addition or removal, perform a full rematch by removing all matches and recomputing them for all targets of the batch. 4. Emit a reload event for the batch users to update the system with the changes, if the operation is flagged as an independent transaction. 5. If there are any failed samples, return them as part of the function’s result to be processed in rematch_batches endpoint

Parameters:

  • sample_batch_id (str) – ID of the sample batch for which the rematch is to be performed.

  • added_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of target compound IDs for which matches need to be computed, defaults to None

  • added_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of ionization mechanism IDs for which matches need to be computed, defaults to None

  • removed_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of target compound IDs for which matches are to be removed, defaults to None

  • removed_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of ionization mechanism IDs for which matches are to be removed, defaults to None

Notes

  • If removed_* parameters are provided, the function removes matches related to these parameters.

  • If added_* parameters are provided, the function computes new matches related to these parameters.

  • If no added_* or removed_* parameters are provided, the function removes all existing matches and computes new matches for all targets.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.rematch_batches(sample_batches: List[MatchComputeBatch], added_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, added_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None)[source]¶

Performs a rematch operation on multiple sample batches, handling both the removal of existing matches and the computation of new matches based on changes in target compounds or ionization mechanisms.

This function iterates over each sample batch, applying removals and additions of matches as specified. It also aggregates any failures across batches for reporting purposes.

Steps: 1. Gather initial data for each sample batch, including the number of items per batch and workspace IDs. 2. Notify clients that batch processing has started. 3. Sequentially process each sample batch, performing removals and additions as specified. 4. Aggregate failed samples from each batch for reporting. 5. Notify clients that batch processing has finished, including information about any failures.

Parameters:

  • sample_batches (List[MatchComputeBatch]) – A list of sample batch identifiers along with optional workspace information.

  • added_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – IDs of target compounds added, for which new matches should be computed.

  • added_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – IDs of ionization mechanisms added, for which new matches should be computed.

  • removed_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – IDs of target compounds removed, for which existing matches should be deleted.

  • removed_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – IDs of ionization mechanisms removed, for which existing matches should be deleted.

Returns:

A status message indicating the outcome of the batch rematch operation, including any failed match computations for samples.

Return type:

dict

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_controller.rematch_sample(sample_item_id: str, added_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, added_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, removed_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, independent_transaction: bool = False)[source]¶

Performs a rematch of sample by removing and/or computing matches based on the specified parameters.

This function handles the rematch process of a sample by first removing matches associated with removed target compounds or ionization mechanisms and then adding matches for added compounds or mechanisms. If no parameters are provided, it performs a complete rematch by removing all existing sample matches and recomputing them.

Steps: 1. Remove existing matches associated with removed parameters, if specified. 2. Compute new matches for added parameters, if specified. 3. In the absence of specified parameters for addition or removal, perform a full rematch by removing all matches and recomputing them for all targets of the sample. 4. Emit a reload event for the sample’s batch to update the system with the changes, if the operation is flagged as an independent transaction.

Parameters:

  • sample_item_id (str) – ID of the sample item for which the rematch is to be performed.

  • added_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of target compound IDs for which matches need to be computed, defaults to None

  • added_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of ionization mechanism IDs for which matches need to be computed, defaults to None

  • removed_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of target compound IDs for which matches are to be removed, defaults to None

  • removed_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – List of ionization mechanism IDs for which matches are to be removed, defaults to None

Notes

  • If removed_* parameters are provided, the function removes matches related to these parameters.

  • If added_* parameters are provided, the function computes new matches related to these parameters.

  • If no added_* or removed_* parameters are provided, the function removes all existing matches and computes new matches for all targets.

backend.api_rest.controllers.match_interferences_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_interferences_controller.create_match_interferences(match_interference_df, sample_item_id)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_interferences_controller.delete_match_interferences(sample_item_ids: List[str], target_isotope_ids: List[str] | None = None)[source]¶

Deletes match interferences for specified sample items, optionally filtered by target isotope IDs.

This function deletes match interferences for a list of sample item IDs. If target isotope IDs are specified, only interferences corresponding to these isotopes are deleted.

Parameters:

  • sample_item_ids (List[str]) – List of sample item IDs for which match interferences are to be deleted.

  • target_isotope_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – Optional list of target isotope IDs to filter match interferences, defaults to None

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_interferences_controller.get_match_interference_by_id(match_interference_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_interferences_controller.get_match_interferences(target_isotope_id: str | None = None, sample_item_id: str | None = None, min_sample_peak_interference: float | None = None, max_sample_peak_interference: float | None = None, sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 1000000)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.match_rating_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_rating_controller.create_match_rating(match_rating: MatchRatingCreate)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_rating_controller.get_match_rating_by_id(match_rating_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.match_rating_controller.get_match_ratings(sample_item_id: str | None = None, target_ion_id: str | None = None, rating: int | None = None, sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 100, minDatetime: datetime | None = None, maxDatetime: datetime | None = None)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.matches_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.matches_controller.create_matches(match_isotope_df, sample_item_id)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.matches_controller.delete_matches(sample_item_ids: List[str], target_isotope_ids: List[str] | None = None)[source]¶

Deletes matches for specified sample items, optionally filtered by target isotope IDs.

This function deletes matches for a list of sample item IDs. If target isotope IDs are provided, only matches corresponding to these isotopes are deleted.

Parameters:

  • sample_item_ids (List[str]) – List of sample item IDs for which matches are to be deleted.

  • target_isotope_ids (Optional[List[str]], optional) – Optional list of target isotope IDs to filter matches, defaults to None

async backend.api_rest.controllers.matches_controller.get_match_by_id(match_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.matches_controller.get_matches(sample_item_id: str | None = None, target_isotope_id: str | None = None, sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 1000000)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.autosampler_import_batch(sample_batch, sample_items, params: CalibrationMzFitParams, background_tasks)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.copy_sample_batch(sample_batch_copy: SampleBatchCopy, sid=None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.create_sample_batch(sample_batch: SampleBatchCreate)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.delete_sample_batch(sample_batch_id: str, sid=None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.get_batch_targets(sample_batch_id: str, alarms_list: List[str] = ['TARGETS'])[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.get_sample_batch(sample_batch_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.get_sample_batches(workspace_id: str, sort: str, order: str, page: int, limit: int)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.process_batch(sample_batch, sample_items, params)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.sample_batch_export_peaks(sample_batch: SampleBatchExportPeaks, sid=None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_batches_controller.update_sample_batch(sample_batch_id: str, sample_batch: SampleBatchUpdate, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks)[source]¶

This function updates a sample batch in the database based on the provided data. It checks for changes in target collections and ionization mechanisms. It determines whether a rematch is needed by comparing the existing and updated target compounds and ionisation mechanisms of the batch. If a rematch is required, it prepares and executes the rematch process using background tasks.

Steps: 1. Fetch the existing sample batch data. 2. Determine if a rematch is needed based on changes in collections or ionization mechanisms. 3. Update the sample batch with new data. 4. Execute rematch if needed, determining added/removed compounds and/or ionization mechanisms. 5. Trigger rematching or emit a workspace reload event based on the need for a rematch.

Parameters:

  • sample_batch_id (str) – ID of the sample batch to be updated.

  • sample_batch (SampleBatchUpdate) – Updated data for the sample batch.

  • background_tasks (BackgroundTasks) – Background tasks for asynchronous execution.

Raises:

HTTPException – Raised if the sample batch is not found in the database.

Returns:

The updated sample batch object.

backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller.create_sample_file(sample_file: SampleFileCreate)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller.delete_sample_file(sample_file_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller.get_sample_file_by_id(sample_file_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller.get_sample_file_peak_timeseries(sample_file_id: str, peak_mz: float, peak_mz_tolerance_ppm: float) → dict[source]¶

Get timeseries of a given peak in a given sample file.

Returns the timeseries of a closest peak to a given m/z, if found within given m/z tolerance.

Parameters:

  • sample_file_id (str) – Sample file ID

  • peak_mz (float) – m/z of the peak to get timeseries for

  • peak_mz_tolerance_ppm (float) – Tolerance for m/z difference for the requested peak and the nearest one found from data

Raises:

HTTPException – Raised if sample file is not found

Returns:

Dictionary with keys: “mz”: m/z of the peak in sample file (None if no peak within tolerance) “intensity”: peak height at time points (empty if no peak within tolerance) “time”: time coordinates (empty if no peak within tolerance)

Return type:

dict

async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller.get_sample_file_peaks(sample_file_id: str) → dict[source]¶

Get peaks of given sample file

Parameters:

sample_file_id (str) – Sample file ID

Raises:

HTTPException – Raised if sample file is not found

Returns:

Dictionary with keys: “mz”: list of m/z of the peaks in sample file “intensity”: peak intensity (area)

Return type:

dict

async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller.get_sample_files(sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 100, minDatetime: datetime | None = None, maxDatetime: datetime | None = None, instrument: str | None = None, filename: str | None = None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_files_controller.update_sample_file(sample_file_id: str, sample_file: SampleFileUpdate)[source]¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_items_controller.get_sample_item(sample_item_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.sample_items_controller.get_sample_items(sample_batch_id: str | None = None, filename: str | None = None, sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 10000)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.aggregate_match_collections(compounds_df: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]¶

Aggregate fields for matchCollections from compounds dataframe.

This function sorts the compounds dataframe by match_category and match_score in descending order and then groups by target_collection_id and other relevant fields to compute the aggregated values for match_score, match_category, sample_peak_area_sum, and sample_peak_interference_sum. It preserves the highest match_score of compound from the most alarming match_category (the most alarming compound in collection) and sums up the sample_peak_area_sum and sample_peak_interference_sum for the entire group.

Parameters:

compounds_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing compound data to aggregate.

Returns:

DataFrame with aggregated matchCollections data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.aggregate_match_compounds(ions_df: ~pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) -> (<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>, <class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>)[source]¶

Aggregate fields for matchCompounds from ions dataframe.

This function sorts the ions dataframe by match_category and match_score in descending order and then groups by target_compound_id and other relevant fields to compute the aggregated values for match_score, match_category, sample_peak_area_sum, and sample_peak_interference_sum. It preserves the highest match_score of ion from the most alarming match_category (the most alarming ion) and sums up the sample_peak_area_sum and sample_peak_interference_sum for the entire group.

It prepares two DataFrames: 1) match_compounds_data_df with detailed data for further aggregation, 2) match_compounds_df with reduced data for frontend display.

Parameters:

ions_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing ion data to aggregate.

Returns:

pandas DataFrame with aggregated matchCompounds data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.aggregate_match_ions(isotopes_df: ~pandas.core.frame.DataFrame, filter_params: ~backend.api_rest.models.pydantic_models.sample_pydantic_model.FilterParams | None = None) -> (<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>, <class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>)[source]¶
Aggregate fields for matchIons from isotopes dataframe.

Provided filters are passed to set_ions_match_category, if none filter_params are provided, stored ion-specidic or default params will be applied.

This function groups the isotopes dataframe by by target_ion_id and other related field.

It prepares two DataFrames: 1) match_ions_data_df with detailed data for further aggregation, 2) match_ions_df with reduced data for frontend display.

The match_score is calculated as a weighted sum of individual isotopes’ match scores, weighted by their relative abundance. The sample_peak_area and sample_peak_interference are summed across all isotopes in the group, the _sum is added to the field name.

Parameters:

  • isotopes_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing isotope data to aggregate.

  • filter_params (Optional[FilterParams]) – Optional ion-specific filter parameters to set_ions_match_category.

Returns:

Tuple of DataFrames with aggregated matchIons data.

Return type:

(pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame)

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.aggregate_match_isotopes(match_filter_df: ~pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) -> (<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>, <class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>)[source]¶

Aggregate fields for matchIsotopes from the provided sample/batch match filter dataframe.

This function processes the sample/batch match filter dataframe to aggregate isotope data. It prepares two DataFrames: 1) match_isotopes_data_df with detailed data for further aggregation, 2) match_isotopes_df with reduced data for frontend display.

Parameters:

match_filter_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing sample/batch match filter data to aggregate.

Returns:

Tuple of DataFrames with aggregated matchIsotopes data.

Return type:

(pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame)

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.aggregate_match_samples(compounds_df: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]¶

Aggregate fields for matchSamples from compounds dataframe.

This function sorts the compounds dataframe by alarm_mode, match_category and match_score in descending order and then groups by sample_item_id and other relevant fields to compute the aggregated values for match_score, match_category, sample_peak_area_sum, and sample_peak_interference_sum. It preserves the highest match_score of compound where alarm_mode and match_category is the highest (the most alarming compound of sample) and sums up the sample_peak_area_sum and sample_peak_interference_sum for the entire group.

Parameters:

compounds_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing compound data to aggregate.

Returns:

pandas DataFrame with aggregated matchSamples data.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.aggregate_params(df: DataFrame) → Series[source]¶

Aggregation function no get the aggregated parameters.

Set match_score, match_category and alarm_mode of the top row (the most alarming row). Sums sample_peak_area_sum/sample_peak_interference_sum for the group.

Parameters:

df (pd.DataFrame) – The DataFrame containing the data to be aggregated.

Returns:

A Pandas Series containing the aggregated values.

Return type:

pd.Series

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.compile_samples_df(samples_df: DataFrame, match_samples_df: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]¶

Compile samples dataframe data (from database SampleView) with aggregated match results.

This function merges the samples dataframe with the aggregated match results from the match_samples dataframe. It adds the match_score, match_category, sample_peak_area_sum, and sample_peak_interference_sum fields to each sample. The ‘matched’ field is calculated to indicate whether the sample has any match results.

The aggregation logic in match_samples_df ensures that each sample’s aggregated fields represent:

  • The highest match_score of compound from the most alarming match_category (the most alarming compound of sample)

  • The sum of sample_peak_area and sample_peak_interference for all compounds of the sample

NaN values in aggregated fields are replaced with 0, indicating no matches or data for those fields.

Parameters:

  • samples_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing original database sample data.

  • match_samples_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing aggregated match results for samples.

Returns:

DataFrame with sample data and aggregated match results.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.get_sample(sample_item_id: str, alarms_list: List[str] | None = None, sample_matches_info: bool = False) → dict[source]¶

Retrieves detailed information for a specific sample, including match data if requested.

This function fetches details for a given sample identified by its sample_item_id. It can optionally calculate and include match data as matchIsotopes, matchIons, matchCompounds, and matchCollections, based on the sample’s association with target entities.

If the sample_matches_info parameter is set to False, the function will return basic sample information without performing match calculations. Default is True for http requests, False when called from other controllers.

Parameters:

  • sample_item_id (str) – Unique identifier for the sample.

  • alarms_list (List[str], optional) – List of alarming collections when fetching sample information, defaults to None

  • sample_matches_info (bool, optional) – Flag to determine if match information should be included, defaults to False.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the sample with the specified item ID is not found.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the sample information. If sample_matches_info is True, additional match data is included.

Return type:

dict

The returned dictionary includes the following keys: - ‘data’: Contains the sample information and, if requested, aggregated match data. - ‘message’: A message indicating the status of the operation.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.get_sample_ion_matches(sample_item_id: str, target_ion_id: str, target_collection_id: str, filter_params: FilterParams, alarms_list: List[str] | None = None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.get_samples(sample_item_id: str | None = None, sample_item_id_active: str | None = None, sample_file_id: str | None = None, sample_batch_id: str | None = None, filename: str | None = None, instrument: str | None = None, sample_item_type: str | None = None, minDatetime: datetime | None = None, maxDatetime: datetime | None = None, sort: str = 'datetime_utc', order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 10000, batch_matches_info: bool = False, match_samples: bool = False, match_compounds: bool = False, match_ions: bool = False, match_isotopes: bool = False, alarms_list: List[str] | None = None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.init_batch_match_filter(sample_batch_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.init_sample_match_filter(sample_item_id: str, filter_params: FilterParams | None = None, target_ion_id: str | None = None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.set_alarm_mode(dataframe: DataFrame, alarms_list: List[str]) → DataFrame[source]¶

Set the alarm_mode field for each entry in the DataFrame based on the list of provided alarms_list.

Parameters:

  • dataframe (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing sample/batch match filter data.

  • alarms_list (List[str]) – List of collection types that should have alarm_mode set to True.

Returns:

DataFrame with alarm_mode field set.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

async backend.api_rest.controllers.samples_controller.set_ions_match_category(match_ions_df: DataFrame, filter_params: FilterParams | None = None) → DataFrame[source]¶

Set the match_category field for each ion in the DataFrame.

This function determines the match_category for each ion based on match score and predefined thresholds. It uses provided filters, if no filters are provided then set the ion-specific filters are used, otherwise defaults used as a fall back thresholds.

Parameters:

  • match_ions_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing ion data with match scores.

  • filter_params (Optional[FilterParams]) – Optional ion-specific filter parameters.

Returns:

DataFrame with match_category field set for each ion.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

backend.api_rest.controllers.target_collection_in_sample_batch_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_compound_in_target_collection_controller.create_target_compound_in_target_collection(target_compounds_in_target_collection: List[TargetCompoundInTargetCollectionBase], session=None)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.target_compounds_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_compounds_controller.create_target_compound(target_compounds: List[TargetCompoundBase], session=None) → dict[source]¶

Function to create a target compount record and derived target ions and isotopes

Parameters:

  • target_compounds (List[TargetCompoundBase]) – List of target compounds to create

  • session (SQLAlchemy.AsyncSession, optional) – Database session, if not given makes an independent transaction, defaults to None

Raises:

RuntimeError – Database is malformed

Returns:

Return created target compounds, skipped compounds (already existing) and message log

Return type:

dict

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_compounds_controller.delete_target_compound(target_compound_id: str, session=None)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_compounds_controller.get_target_compound_by_id(target_compound_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_compounds_controller.get_target_compounds(target_compound_name: str | None = None, target_compound_formula: str | None = None, sample_batch_id: str | None = None, sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 1000000)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_compounds_controller.update_target_compound(target_compounds: List[TargetCompoundUpdate])[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.target_ions_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_ions_controller.create_target_ions(target_compound: TargetCompoundBase, ionization_mechanisms: List[IonizationMechanism], target_compound_mass: float | None = None, session=None) → dict[source]¶

Function to create target ion and target isotope records derived from a given target compound and list of ionization mechanisms to apply. If target compound mass is given, it will be used instead of compound formula.

Parameters:

  • target_compound (TargetCompoundBase) – Target compound to derive ions and isotopes from

  • ionization_mechanisms (List[IonizationMechanism]) – List of ionization mechanisms to apply to the compound

  • target_compound_mass – Mass of the target compound (if formula is not known),

defaults to None. If None, formula will be used. :type target_compound_mass: float, optional :param session: Database session, if not given makes an independent transaction, defaults to None :type session: SQLAlchemy.AsyncSession, optional :return: Return created target ions and isotopes, and message log :rtype: dict

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_ions_controller.get_target_ion(target_ion_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_ions_controller.get_target_ions(target_compound_id: str, ionization_mechanism_id: str, target_ion_formula: str, sort: str, order: str, page: int, limit: int)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_ions_controller.update_target_ion(target_ion_id: str, target_ion_update: TargetIonUpdate)[source]¶

backend.api_rest.controllers.target_isotopes_controller module¶

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_isotopes_controller.get_target_isotope(target_isotope_id: str)[source]¶
async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_isotopes_controller.get_target_isotopes(target_ion_id: str | None = None, min_mz: float | None = None, max_mz: float | None = None, min_relative_abundance: float | None = None, max_relative_abundance: float | None = None, target_compound_ids: List[str] | None = None, ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None = None, sample_batch_id: str | None = None, sort: str | None = None, order: str | None = None, page: int = 0, limit: int = 1000000)[source]¶

Retrieves a list of target isotopes based on various filters including target ion ID, mz range, relative abundance range, target compounds, and ionization mechanisms.

Parameters:

  • target_ion_id – Target ion ID filter.

  • min_mz – Minimum m/z filter.

  • max_mz – Maximum m/z filter.

  • min_relative_abundance – Minimum relative abundance filter.

  • max_relative_abundance – Maximum relative abundance filter.

  • target_compound_ids – List of target compound IDs for filtering.

  • ionization_mechanism_ids – List of ionization mechanism IDs for filtering.

  • sample_batch_id – ID of the sample batch for filtering.

  • sort – Sorting field.

  • order – Sorting order (‘asc’ or ‘desc’).

  • page – Pagination page.

  • limit – Number of items per page.

Returns:

Dict with total count and list of target isotopes.

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_isotopes_controller.get_target_isotopes_for_match_compute(batch_target_compounds_ids: List[str], batch_ion_mechanisms_ids: List[str], added_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None, added_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None) → Tuple[List[dict], str][source]¶

Retrieves a list of unique target isotope IDs that are affected by the addition of specific compounds or ionization mechanisms to a sample batch. Adds a description of applied filters. This function helps in identifying the isotopes that require new matches computation after the update in batch composition.

Steps: 1. Fetch isotopes related to added target compounds. 2. Fetch isotopes related to added ionization mechanisms. 3. Combine and deduplicate the isotope IDs from both sources. 4. Create a description of applied filters based on the retrieved data.

Parameters:

  • batch_target_compounds_ids (List[str]) – List of target compound IDs already associated with the batch.

  • batch_ion_mechanisms_ids (List[str]) – List of ionization mechanism IDs already associated with the batch.

  • added_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – Optional list of added target compound IDs.

  • added_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – Optional list of added ionization mechanism IDs.

Returns:

A tuple containing a list of unique target isotope IDs and a string describing the applied filters.

Return type:

Tuple[List[dict], str]

async backend.api_rest.controllers.target_isotopes_controller.get_target_isotopes_for_match_remove(removed_target_compound_ids: List[str] | None, removed_ionization_mechanism_ids: List[str] | None) → Tuple[List[str], str][source]¶

Retrieves a list of unique target isotope IDs that are affected by the removal of specific compounds or ionization mechanisms. Get ALL the assosiated isotopes, not filtering by sample_batch_id. This function aids in identifying the isotopes that should no longer have matches associated with them after the update in the batch composition. Adds a description of applied filters.

Steps: 1. Fetch isotopes related to removed target compounds. 2. Fetch isotopes related to removed ionization mechanisms. 3. Combine and deduplicate the isotope IDs from both sources. 4. Create a description of applied filters based on the retrieved data.

Parameters:

  • removed_target_compound_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – Optional list of removed target compound IDs.

  • removed_ionization_mechanism_ids (Optional[List[str]]) – Optional list of removed ionization mechanism IDs.

Returns:

A tuple containing a list of unique target isotope IDs and a string describing the applied filters.

Return type:

Tuple[List[str], str]
